text: Meet SBI Pulse — our submission for Pillar 3, Digital Engagement, at the SBI Hackathon 2026. An agentic AI that runs proactive engagement journeys — not nudges.

The problem: SBI has around 100 million digital users, but 34 percent of new accounts go dormant within a year, and cross-sell sits at just 2.3 products per customer. Banks blast campaigns; nobody listens. Retaining a customer is 5 to 7 times cheaper than acquiring one — engagement is the highest R O I lever the bank has.

Here is how Pulse works, end to end. Transactions flow in. The system senses signals. A live AI agent reasons about the customer's situation. A deterministic governance gate checks consent and compliance. Only then is a message delivered. The customer replies, and every outcome feeds a learning policy. Let's follow one real customer through this pipeline.
This is Meena. The system reads her actual transaction stream — an income dip, credit cards paid at minimum due, new E M I obligations, and a falling balance. These are signals of financial stress.

Now the AI reasons — this is its own reading of her data: Meena is managing high fixed costs like rent and E M Is, leading to a very low savings rate and signs of financial stress. So instead of selling her a product, it chooses the Stress Shield journey — and suppresses all cross-sell. Knowing when NOT to sell is a decision our agent makes.

Before anything is sent, a deterministic governance gate — plain code, not the AI — verifies D P D P consent, the TRAI nine-to-nine contact window, and frequency caps. The AI proposes; policy disposes. Every step lands on an explainability audit trail.

The message is generated fresh, in Meena's own language — Hindi. And this is a real conversation: she can tap a quick reply or type anything, and the agent responds in character, in her language, and decides when the journey completes — or hands high-value cases to a human relationship manager.
Operations teams see the whole fleet in the Engagement Cockpit — engagement, churn and dormancy scores for every customer, which journeys are running, and what the compliance gate suppressed.
Does it work? We measure uplift against a randomised holdout — not clicks. Twenty percentage points more positive outcomes than business as usual, across a fifteen-hundred customer cohort.

And here is the moat. A Thompson-sampling policy learns which tone works for which segment, from every outcome. Live journeys feed it — so using the product trains it. It climbs from a random baseline to over 90 percent of the theoretical ceiling. The demo is reproducible; this compounding flywheel is not.

SBI Pulse. Journeys, not nudges. It knows when not to sell. Consent and explainability by design. And it learns from every outcome — at a cost of less than five paise per AI conversation. Built for Pillar 3, Digital Engagement. Thank you.
